content: APPROVE WITH CHANGES. 2 blocking: (B1) ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION declared in manifest but no UI gate exists — remove until High Precision toggle is implemented, (B2) sync boundary test is comment-only, not executable — needs real assertion on upsert map keys. 3 advisory: (A1) getLastKnownPosition accepts arbitrarily stale cache, (A2) SharedPreferences toggle is unencrypted XML, (A3) accuracy field is raw/unrounded (local-only). Strengths: opt-in default, rounding at acquisition layer, sync boundary explicit and commented, fire-and-forget with stale-session guard, dual Exception/Error catch, clear-data syncs nulls via PENDING.

APPROVE WITH CHANGES. 2 medium: (F1) _defaultGetCurrentPosition uses generic LocationSettings not AndroidSettings — undocumented platform decision, (F2) sync boundary test is comment-only not executable — ADR-0019 promises a load-bearing test. 3 low: (F3) _captureLocationAsync doc comment has mismatched first sentence, (F4) migration guards use if-from-less-than pattern (inherited, not new), (F5) settings_screen accesses sessionDao directly (existing pattern). Strengths: injectable callables faithfully applied, fire-and-forget correctly implemented, privacy rounding in right layer, clearAllLocationData WHERE clause targeted, LocationEnabledNotifier mirrors VoiceModeNotifier exactly, sync boundary is explicit.

APPROVE WITH CHANGES. 5 low findings: (F1) session_list test adds locationName but never asserts location icon renders, (F2) session_detail_screen has no test for location chip, (F3) sync boundary test is comment-only not executable, (F4) fire-and-forget tests use timing-based 100ms delay — minor flaky risk, (F5) settings location tests are render-only with no interaction tests. Missing edge cases: resumeSession does not re-capture location (intentional but untested), whileInUse as initial checkPermission result not explicitly tested, stale lastKnownPosition accuracy accepted without test documenting the decision. Strengths: injectable callables enable clean testing, never-throws contract verified for both Exception and Error, proper test isolation with in-memory databases, boundary values for coordinates tested, PayloadCaptureSyncRepository is well-designed testing seam.

APPROVE WITH CHANGES. Hidden assumptions: (1) Android geocoder returns locality as primary city field — subLocality ignored, (2) 2s GPS timeout has battery implications on frequent journalers, (3) SharedPreferences survives Auto Backup restore — location_enabled could silently re-enable, (4) locationName strings assumed stable over time, (5) Google geocoder requests are linkable to authenticated Google account — disclosure understates this, (6) stale session guard works for Dart single-thread but discard-then-update could write to deleted row (silent 0-row update), (7) accuracy field is stored but never displayed — creates undocumented data. Pre-mortem: backup restore re-enables location, Google correlates geocoder requests to user, stale cache produces wrong location, cloud null propagation requires manual sync. Alternative considered: name-only storage (no coordinates) would eliminate local leakage risk. Alternative: capture at session end not start.

APPROVE WITH CHANGES. 2 high: (F1) clearAllLocationData has no loading indicator between dialog close and SnackBar, (F2) permission request fires at session start not toggle time — decouples cause from effect, confuses first-time users, deniedForever toggle shows on but nothing recorded. 3 medium: (F3) session card location icon is 14dp with no label or tooltip — users cannot tell what it means, (F4) Clear Location Data button always active even when no data exists — false affordance, (F5) privacy disclosure styled as bodySmall onSurfaceVariant — too small for consent-critical text. 2 low: (F6) session card location icon missing Semantics for screen readers, (F7) clear dialog copy says 'on next sync' but does not explain when that happens. Strengths: confirmation dialog uses correct M3 destructive pattern, context.mounted checks after async, session detail Chip is well-chosen, fire-and-forget honors latency contract, SnackBar handles zero-sessions edge case.

Verdict: APPROVE WITH CHANGES. 4 blocking findings, 12 advisory. Cross-specialist convergence on sync boundary test weakness (3/5 specialists flagged). B1: Remove ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION from manifest (no gating UI). B2: Make sync boundary test executable via @visibleForTesting buildSessionUpsertMap. B3: Add loading indicator for clearAllLocationData. B4: Request permission at toggle-on time, not session start. Advisory findings include session card accessibility, disclosure text prominence, stale cache acceptance, and missing interaction tests. Strengths unanimously recognized: injectable callables pattern, privacy rounding at acquisition, fire-and-forget with guards, sync boundary design, opt-in default.
